identify_tls_entry.expected_solution: Agent identifies git's HTTP/TLS configuration entry points The answer must reference the canonical artifact(s) for this checkpoint at the pinned revision (basenames: http.c, openssl.c, x509_vfy.c, http.h, and urldata.h). The judge looks for the following concept tokens in the agent output: http.c, http.sslCAInfo, sslCAInfo.
evaluation_criteria: Cites at least one canonical file by basename (http.c, openssl.c, x509_vfy.c, http.h, and urldata.h) in the agent output
Mentions at least three of the canonical concept tokens: http.c, http.sslCAInfo, and sslCAInfo

trace_curl_integration.expected_solution: Agent traces curl's OpenSSL backend configuration path The answer must reference the canonical artifact(s) for this checkpoint at the pinned revision (basenames: openssl.c and urldata.h).
evaluation_criteria: Cites at least one canonical file by basename (openssl.c and urldata.h) in the agent output
References the checkpoint goal as described in task.toml: Agent traces curl's OpenSSL backend configuration path
Connects the source artifacts to their downstream impact in the consumer code path

openssl_verification.expected_solution: Agent identifies OpenSSL X509 verification chain and error origin The answer must reference the canonical artifact(s) for this checkpoint at the pinned revision (basenames: x509_vfy.c). The judge looks for the following concept tokens in the agent output: x509_vfy, X509_verify_cert.
evaluation_criteria: Cites at least one canonical file by basename (x509_vfy.c) in the agent output
Mentions at least three of the canonical concept tokens: x509_vfy and X509_verify_cert
Connects the source artifacts to their downstream impact in the consumer code path
